title: gaultheria depressa
intro: Gaultheria depressa, also known as the lowland cranberry or New Zealand cranberry, is a small, woody shrub native to New Zealand and Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of organic acids, including gallic acid, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Gaultheria depressa include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. Gaultheria depressa is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions.
